text: During the laparoscopic protac rectopexy, the surgeon mobilizes the rectum down to the pelvic floor using the total mesorectal excision (TME) technique. The peritoneum is divided bilaterally, with the rectum appearing mobile on the right side. The division of the peritoneum on the patient's left side is completed.

The surgeon releases the peritoneal attachments of the sigmoid colon, straightening it out. The rectum is visibly mobile, and the peritoneum is secured to the sacral promontory with three bilateral sutures. The rectum is then hitched onto the sacral promontory, resolving the prolapse.
